rubric_en: How David Sacks and the new tech right went full MAGA and captured Washington
rubric_zh: 戴维·萨克斯与“新科技右派”如何全面走向MAGA并占领华盛顿

paragraphs.en: The courtship between Silicon Valley and MAGA was consummated on June 6, 2024, in San Francisco’s Pacific Heights neighborhood, on a street known as “Billionaires’ Row,” at the 22,000-square-foot, $45 million French-limestone mansion of a venture capitalist named David Sacks. Along with Chamath Palihapitiya, a fellow venture capitalist and a colleague on the All-In podcast, Sacks hosted a fundraiser for Donald Trump. He knew that other technology titans were coming around to the ex-president but remained in the closet. “And I think that this event is going to break the ice on that,” Sacks said on the podcast the week before the fundraiser. “And maybe it’ll create a preference cascade, where all of a sudden it becomes acceptable to acknowledge the truth.”
paragraphs.zh: 硅谷与“美国优先”阵营的“联姻”在2024年6月6日画上句号。地点在旧金山太平洋高地区一条被称为“亿万富翁大道”的街道上，地点是风险投资家大卫·萨克斯在该处的一栋2.2万平方英尺、耗资4500万美元的法国白垩石庄园。萨克斯与同为风险投资人、也是“全力开讲”播客同事的查玛斯·帕利哈皮蒂亚一道为唐纳德·特朗普举办了筹款活动。他知道，其他科技巨头也都在转向这位前总统，但还在“躲躲闪闪”。“我想这次活动会打破那层尴尬，”萨克斯在筹款会前一周的播客里说。“也许它会引发一种偏好连锁反应——突然之间，承认真相就会变得可以被接受。”

paragraphs.en: A few years earlier, Sacks had described the January 6, 2021, riot at the U.S. Capitol as an “insurrection” and pronounced Trump “disqualified” from ever again holding national office. “What Trump did was absolutely outrageous, and I think it brought him to an ignominious end in American politics,” he said on the podcast a few days after the event. “He will pay for it in the history books, if not in a court of law.” Palihapitiya was more colloquial, calling Trump “a complete piece-of-shit fucking scumbag.” These might seem like tricky positions to climb down from—but the path that leads from scathing denunciation through gradual accommodation to sycophantic embrace of Trump is a well-worn pilgrimage trail. The journey is less wearisome for self-mortifiers who never considered democracy (a word seldom spoken on the podcast) all that important in the first place. One prominent traveler who had already shown the way was a guest at the fundraiser—Senator J. D. Vance, whose attendance helped close the deal on his selection as Trump’s running mate. Any lingering awkwardness between the hosts and their guest of honor was dispelled by the fundraiser’s $12 million haul, much of it from cryptocurrency moguls.
paragraphs.zh: 几年前，萨克斯在1月6日2021年美国国会大厦骚乱发生后把这次事件称为“叛乱”，并断言特朗普已经“被取消资格”，永远不能再次担任全国性公职。“特朗普所做的是绝对过分的事情，我想这把他送进了美国政治的耻辱结局。”他在事件几天后在播客中说。“如果不是在法庭上，他也会在史书里为此付出代价。”帕利哈皮蒂亚的说法更口语化，他称特朗普是“一个彻头彻尾的烂透了的渣滓。”这两种表态看似都很难从中退身——但从严厉谴责，到逐步妥协，再到对特朗普的奉承式拥抱，这条路其实是条走得很熟的“朝圣”路线。对那些从一开始就不太在意民主（这个词在播客里很少被提及）的人来说，这趟旅程更毫不费力。其中一位早已示范过路径的“名人旅人”就是这场筹款会的来宾——参议员J·D·万斯；而且他的出席帮助敲定了他出任特朗普竞选搭档的决定。主办方与这位贵宾之间若有任何尴尬，都被这场筹款会1,200万美元的收入彻底化解，资金很大一部分来自加密货币大亨。

paragraphs.en: Opportunist doesn’t really describe Sacks. He doesn’t come across as slippery or two-faced. There’s no evasive glance or roguish smile. He can argue at great length, in a steady sinal drone, with an aggressive debater’s ability to make an evidence-based case for any position he holds—but the position always happens to coincide with his benefit. The only consistent principle of his career is a ruthless devotion to self-interest. Sacks has identified as a “libertarian conservative” all of his adult life, but he has sought government intervention on behalf of his investments when it’s suited him. In 2023, when Silicon Valley Bank collapsed, Sacks demanded that the federal government bail out the uninsured deposits of start-up companies, much of the money from crypto firms. “Some libertarians care about the freedom of only one person,” Peter Thiel, the entrepreneur, investor, and right-wing provocateur, once said of his friend Sacks.
paragraphs.zh: 说萨克斯是机会主义者并不贴切。他并不油滑，也不两面派。你看不出他有躲闪的眼神或奸诈的笑意。他能以稳定、单调而有力的语调长时间地辩论，并且有一位咄咄逼人的辩手那样，为任何自己当前的立场建立起看似有据可依的论证——但那个立场往往恰好与他的个人利益一致。他职业生涯里唯一稳定的原则，就是对自身利益的无情奉献。萨克斯一生都把自己定义为“自由意志保守主义者”，但当有利可图时，他会寻求政府干预来扶持自己的投资。2023年，当硅谷银行倒闭时，他要求联邦政府救助创业公司里未获保险的存款，其中很多钱来自加密企业。“有些自由意志主义者只在乎一个人的自由，”企业家、投资人、右翼煽动者彼得·蒂尔曾经这样评价他的朋友萨克斯。

paragraphs.en: From the May 2026 issue: What Noah Hawley learned about billionaires at Jeff Bezos’s private retreat
paragraphs.zh: 出自2026年5月刊：诺亚·霍利在杰夫·贝索斯私人度假地学到的关于亿万富翁的故事

paragraphs.en: In this sense, though Trump is impulsive and narcissistic while Sacks is cold-eyed and logical, they are well matched. “Sacks is a spirit animal for part of the president’s brain,” a former Biden-administration official told me. “The plutocratic part.” After the election, the new president appointed Sacks as his special adviser, or “czar,” for AI and crypto. After decades of keeping as far from Washington as possible, Silicon Valley would finally have its own man in the White House.
paragraphs.zh: 从这个意义上说，尽管特朗普冲动又自恋，而萨克斯则冷静理性、逻辑清晰，他们却非常般配。一位前拜登政府官员对我说：“萨克斯是总统大脑里的一种精神动物，‘那个资产阶级化的部分。’”大选之后，新任总统任命萨克斯担任其在人工智能和加密货币领域的特别顾问，也就是“太监/宰相”式的角色。在几十年来尽量与华盛顿保持距离之后，硅谷终于在白宫有了自己的人。

paragraphs.en: But Sacks has always taken a dim view of politics. At 25, appearing on a C-SPAN talk show while still in law school, he expressed a preference for “the ethos of Wall Street” over “the ethos of Washington” and quoted Calvin Coolidge on the business of America being business, avowing: “I’d probably rather live in a greedy country where people don’t share than in an envious country where people are stealing from each other.”
paragraphs.zh: 但萨克斯一直对政治持悲观看法。在25岁、仍在法学院上学期间参加C-SPAN谈话节目时，他表达了自己偏爱“华尔街精神”而不是“华盛顿精神”，并引用加尔文·柯立芝的话，说美国的事业是生意，并宣称：“我宁愿生活在一个贪婪、但人们不互相攀比的国家，也不愿生活在一个充满嫉妒、人人互相窃取的国家。”

paragraphs.en: Sacks went to Washington on behalf of business, including his own. But business and politics demand different, sometimes opposing talents. “Sacks’s policies are misaligned with his own party,” a congressional aide with a close view of how Sacks operates in Washington told me. “He doesn’t really understand how D.C. works.” His efforts in government on behalf of the tech industry have exposed the president to the charge that Trump is selling out his populist base on behalf of the country’s richest men, driving a wedge through the MAGA coalition.
paragraphs.zh: 萨克斯前来华盛顿，是以商业，尤其是他自己的商业利益为名。可商业与政治需要不同、甚至有时相互对立的才能。一位接近萨克斯运作方式并在国会任职的助理对我说：“萨克斯的政策与他所在的政党并不一致，‘他的政策与他的党不对口。’”他在政府中替科技行业发声的做法，使总统面临这样的指责：特朗普正在以美国最富有的人为代价，出卖他的民粹基础，从而在MAGA联盟内部撕开了一道裂缝。

paragraphs.en: Sacks once called a rare victory over Thiel in a game of chess one of the greatest moments of his life. In a photo, his arms are raised skyward, ecstatic disbelief on his face. He spent the early years of his career as a kind of junior partner in Thiel’s shadow. Sacks was born in 1972 in South Africa, and moved to the United States at age 5. He grew up in Memphis and attended an elite boys’ prep school before going on to Stanford University. As a sophomore with right-wing views he inevitably gravitated toward Thiel, who was by then in law school, and joined The Stanford Review, the conservative campus publication that Thiel had started as an undergrad. It took aim at the politically correct orthodoxy and anti-Western ideology that swept over American higher education in the late ’80s and early ’90s and never really left. But the outnumbered young conservatives’ mockery almost always overshot the target. An entire issue was devoted to making light of rape, including a contribution from Sacks that challenged whether statutory rape should be a crime. (He has since expressed regret for some of his youthful writings.)
paragraphs.zh: 萨克斯曾说，在一盘棋局中罕见战胜塞尔（Thiel）是他一生中最伟大的时刻之一。有一张照片里，他双臂高举，脸上是难以置信的狂喜。职业生涯早期，他像个小搭档一样在赛尔的阴影下成长。萨克斯1972年出生于南非，5岁移民美国，在孟菲斯长大后进入一所精英男子寄宿中学，随后就读斯坦福大学。作为一名持右翼观点的大二学生，他顺理成章地向赛尔靠拢；当时赛尔已在法学院就读，于是萨克斯加入了《斯坦福评论》（The Stanford Review）——那是赛尔大学时期创办的保守主义校园刊物。它直指80年代末到90年代初席卷美国高等教育并始终未曾真正退出的政治正确正统观念和反西方意识形态。但这群人数不足的年轻保守派常常将讽刺开成了靶子。某一期整版杂志都在把性侵犯问题轻描淡写，其中甚至刊登了萨克斯的文章，质疑法定强奸是否应当构成犯罪。他后来为自己一些年轻时的文字表达过遗憾。

paragraphs.en: Thiel was determined to be a public intellectual like his hero William F. Buckley, so he began writing a book on left-wing campus extremism. When he found the work too onerous, he turned the research over to Sacks, and they co-authored The Diversity Myth: Multiculturalism and Political Intolerance on Campus, published in 1995 by a libertarian think tank. Sacks attended the University of Chicago Law School, but law was too much like the detested public sector, and in 1999, when Thiel co-founded an online-payments company in Palo Alto that was soon to be called PayPal, Sacks left a consulting job to lead the company’s product team. He made important contributions to PayPal’s success; by various accounts, including Sacks’s own, he was also known for telling co-workers in blunt terms that they were wrong. A former colleague told me that with Sacks, “there’s masters and there’s slaves. He doesn’t have partners: ‘You do what I tell you to do, or you’re one of the few people that tell me what you want me to do.’ ” The former colleague added, “Part of his drive is that he believes he is one of the small number of elite people who really get it and are capable.” (The former colleague and some other Silicon Valley sources requested anonymity to discuss a figure who has power over their businesses; some government officials requested anonymity to speak about White House conversations, because they were not authorized to talk about them. Sacks declined to be interviewed.)
paragraphs.zh: 蒂尔决心成为一位公共知识分子，像他崇拜的威廉·F·巴克利一样，于是他开始写一本关于左翼校园极端主义的书。后来他觉得这项工作太繁重，就把研究交给萨克斯，两人合著了《多样性神话：校园中的多元文化主义与政治不宽容》。该书于1995年由一家自由主义智库出版。萨克斯就读于芝加哥大学法学院，但他觉得律师工作太像他厌恶的公共部门，1999年当蒂尔在帕洛阿尔托联合创办一家不久将被命名为PayPal的网络支付公司时，萨克斯辞去咨询工作，带领该公司的产品团队。他对PayPal的成功做出了重要贡献；在包括萨克斯自己在内的多方说法里，他也以对同事直言不讳地指出其错误而闻名。一位前同事告诉我，在萨克斯那里，“有主人，也有奴隶。你没有合伙人关系：‘你要么照我说的去做，要么你就是那少数告诉我你想让我做什么的人。’” 那位前同事补充道，“他有一股驱动力，因为他相信自己是少数真正懂行且有能力的精英之一。”（这位前同事和其他几位硅谷消息人士要求匿名，以便讨论一个对他们企业有影响力的人物；一些政府官员也请求匿名，因为他们没有被授权谈论白宫对话。萨克斯拒绝接受采访。）

paragraphs.en: PayPal became famous for surviving the dot-com crash in 2000, and for producing a spawn of Silicon Valley stars known as the PayPal Mafia, including Sacks. Roger McNamee, a longtime tech investor, watched its success with admiration and apprehension. The PayPal Mafia saw before anyone else that the cost of starting an internet company was going to drop significantly. “They realized that the limits on processing power were going to go away,” McNamee told me. But these 20- and 30-somethings were not inspired in the same way that the founders of earlier Silicon Valley companies were: “They didn’t follow the vision of Steve Jobs, that tech can democratize power. They came to get rich.” McNamee added, “If their value system had been different, we would have a completely different country today.”
paragraphs.zh: PayPal因在2000年互联网泡沫破裂中幸存下来而闻名，也因培养出一批被称为“PayPal黑帮”——包括萨克斯在内的硅谷明星而出名。长期以来的科技投资人罗杰·麦克纳米欣喜地、又带着忧虑地观察到它的成功。PayPal黑帮在别人之前就看出了创建互联网公司成本即将大幅下降。“他们意识到，处理能力的瓶颈将会消失，”麦克纳米向我表示道。但这些20岁和30岁出头的人并未像硅谷早期公司创始人那样受到同样的理想驱动： “他们并不追随史蒂夫·乔布斯的愿景——科技可以让权力下放。他们的目标是赚钱。” 麦克纳米又补充说，“如果他们的价值观不同，我们今天会有一张完全不同的国家样貌。”

paragraphs.en: I met Sacks in 2011, at a dinner at Thiel’s house in San Francisco with a small group of entrepreneurs and investors, most of them PayPal alumni. They despised higher education, worshipped the creators of tech companies, wanted to found libertarian colonies on the high seas and be cryogenically frozen for future resurrection—eccentric outliers then, but forerunners of a broader political trend in the Valley. One guest was an AI expert named Eliezer Yudkowsky. Last year, he co-authored If Anyone Builds It, Everyone Dies, which concludes that artificial superintelligence will kill literally every human being on Earth—thereby causing Thiel to label him “a legionnaire of the Antichrist.”
paragraphs.zh: 我在2011年遇见了萨克斯，那是在旧金山蒂尔家的一次晚宴上，小规模地集结了许多创业者和投资人，其中大多数是PayPal校友。他们蔑视高等教育，膜拜科技公司的创始人，想在公海上建立自由主义殖民地，并希望被冷冻保存以期待未来复生——当时这是些怪异的离群者，但也是硅谷更广泛政治潮流的先行者。一位名叫伊莱泽·尤德科夫斯基的人工智能专家也在场。去年，他与人合著了《If Anyone Builds It, Everyone Dies》（《只要有人建造，它就会结束，所有人都将死去》），书中得出结论：人工超级智能会杀死地球上每一个人，因此蒂尔称他为“反基督的军团士兵”。

paragraphs.en: Read: AI is grown, not built
paragraphs.zh: 阅读：AI是成长出来的，不是被建造出来的

paragraphs.en: Sacks seemed the most normal of the group. He was a businessman with conventional libertarian views, more optimistic than Thiel about the economic power of the internet, less apocalyptic about the decline and fall of “Western civilization,” a key term in The Diversity Myth that Sacks seldom used after publication, showing no consistent ideological attachment other than to capitalism. His distaste for politics remained strong. “This is the battle,” Sacks told me. “Can the web disrupt the rest of the economy, or does the old economy fight back using politics to keep the new economy from taking over?” At the time we spoke, he was trying to disrupt the car-wash business. He had invested in an app that allowed you to send your car’s location to a person who would come wash it while you were off getting sushi or founding a company or taking a meeting in Hong Kong. The app, called Cherry, lasted only a year, but Sacks did better with another early-stage investment in a company that sent a town car to pick you up. “It’s totally disrupted the taxi business,” Sacks said of Uber, with undisguised pleasure.
paragraphs.zh: 在那群人里，萨克斯看起来最“正常”。他是个有着传统自由主义观点的商人，对互联网的经济力量比蒂尔更乐观，也不太像“文明衰败”的悲观论者，后者是萨克斯在《多样性神话》（The Diversity Myth）中常见的“西方文明”式表述，但书出版后他很少再使用这个说法，除了资本主义之外，他没有表现出一致的意识形态立场。他对政治的厌恶依然很强。他对我说： “这就是战争：网络能否重构整个经济，还是旧经济会利用政治反击，从而阻止新经济接管？” 我们交谈时，他正试图颠覆洗车行业。他投资了一个应用程序，允许你把爱车位置发给别人，在你外出吃寿司、创办公司或在香港开会时由他人上门洗车。这个名为 Cherry 的应用只活了一年，但他在另一家早期项目中投资得更成功——那家公司会派豪华出租车来接你。“这是对出租车行业的彻底颠覆，”他说起 Uber 时毫不掩饰地露出欣喜。

paragraphs.en: He did extremely well, with a movie he co-produced in 2005 (Thank You for Smoking ), with a company he co-founded in 2008 (a Slack-like social network for businesses called Yammer), and with his investments: in Facebook, Palantir, and SpaceX after PayPal was sold to eBay for $1.5 billion in 2002; in bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies after he sold Yammer to Microsoft for $1.2 billion in 2012. That year, he threw himself a Marie Antoinette–themed 40th birthday party in a rented ancien régime–style Los Angeles mansion, with special guest Snoop Dogg. “Part of believing in capitalism is you don’t have to feel guilty,” Sacks told me.
paragraphs.zh: 2005 年，他与人合制的电影《谢谢你抽烟》最初让他收益颇丰；2008 年他联合创办的公司（名为 Yammer 的类 Slack 商业社交网络）也很成功；在投资方面更是如此：PayPal 于 2002 年以 15 亿美元卖给 eBay 后，他参与投资了 Facebook、Palantir 和 SpaceX；2012 年他以 12 亿美元将 Yammer 卖给微软后，又押注比特币等加密货币。那一年，他在洛杉矶一处租来的仿旧制度风格庄园里，举办了一场玛丽·安托瓦内特主题的 40 岁生日会，并邀请了嘉宾 Snoop Dogg。“相信资本主义的一部分意思是你不必感到内疚，”萨克斯对我说。

paragraphs.en: David Sacks and Elon Musk attend a party after a screening of the 2005 film Thank You for Smoking, which they co-produced, at Elaine’s in New York City. (Christian Grattan / Patrick McMullan / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 大卫·萨克斯与伊隆·马斯克在纽约市 Elaine’s 共同出席一场放映后派对。影片是他们共同制作的 2005 年电影《谢谢你抽烟》，（摄影：Christian Grattan / Patrick McMullan / Getty）。

paragraphs.en: He conducted himself in the usual way of an aristocrat of the second Gilded Age: buying lavish properties, contributing to mainstream politicians (Mitt Romney in 2012, Hillary Clinton in 2016), and guarding his family’s privacy. He deplored the deterioration of urban life and funded the recall of San Francisco’s ultraprogressive district attorney, Chesa Boudin. Unlike Thiel, he didn’t publish writings on reactionary philosophers and the virtues of monopolistic capitalism.
paragraphs.zh: 他的行为方式很像“第二次镀金时代”的贵族：购买奢华地产、向主流政治人物捐款（2012 年支持米特·罗姆尼，2016 年支持希拉里·克林顿），并严守家庭隐私。他谴责城市生活的恶化，并资助了旧金山极左检察官查萨·布丁的罢免投票。与蒂尔不同，他没有发表过关于反动哲学家和垄断资本主义美德的著作。

paragraphs.en: The politics of the Valley was always a liberal sort of libertarianism: pro-choice, pro-immigration, idealistic, even utopian, arrogant about its mission of empowering individuals and connecting humanity, but indifferent to and ignorant of government, with an engineer’s contempt for the creaky workings of bureaucracy and the cluelessness of elected officials. Leave us alone to do our magic, which you can’t possibly understand, and everyone will benefit.
paragraphs.zh: 硅谷的政治长期以来一直是一种自由主义式的自由主义：支持堕胎权、支持移民、充满理想主义，甚至带点乌托邦色彩，在赋权个人与连接全人类的使命感中略带自负，却对政府漠不关心、甚至一无所知，对政府机关运转的陈旧僵化怀有工程师式的轻蔑，对民选官员的缺乏判断力也几乎不屑一顾。让我们自己来做点‘魔法’吧，这些事你根本不可能理解，人人都会受益。

paragraphs.en: But about a decade ago, tech’s free ride ran into trouble. In 2013 Marc Andreessen, an inventor of the first popular web browser in the ’90s and now one of the Valley’s most successful venture capitalists, predicted to me a public backlash against technology companies over privacy rights, intellectual property, and monopoly power. With more foresight he would have included the addictive and corrosive effects of social media. Three years later, in 2016, Facebook enabled Russian meddling in an election that inflamed American divisions and sent Trump to the White House.
paragraphs.zh: 但大约十年前，科技行业的“顺风局”遇到了麻烦。2013年，马克·安德森——他是上世纪90年代第一款流行网络浏览器的发明者之一，如今也是硅谷最成功的风险投资家之一——在我面前预言，科技公司将因隐私权、知识产权和垄断权力问题而遭遇公众反弹。若他更有先见之明，还会把社交媒体的成瘾性和腐蚀性也算进去。三年后，也就是2016年，脸书促成了俄罗斯干预大选，激化了美国的分裂，并把特朗普送进白宫。

paragraphs.en: Trump and his populist followers made Big Tech a favorite target; so did progressives such as Senator Elizabeth Warren. Under bipartisan pressure, Silicon Valley had to search for ways to keep the government out of its business. Executives and investors spent fortunes on lobbying and campaign contributions. Mark Zuckerberg showed up in Washington to stand before Congress with his hand raised—eyes wide, as if stunned by the reality of representative government—and explain in tortured sentences why Facebook’s platforms weren’t driving America’s children to anxiety and depression while shredding the country’s civic ligature.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普和他的民粹追随者把大型科技企业当成了最爱的攻击目标；进步派人物如参议员伊丽莎白·沃伦也是如此。在两党施压下，硅谷不得不寻找让政府远离其业务的方法。高管与投资者在游说和竞选捐款上花费巨资。马克·扎克伯格来到华盛顿，在国会面前举起手，双眼圆睁，仿佛被代表制政府的现实惊住了，艰难地解释为何脸书平台并没有把美国孩子推向焦虑和抑郁，也没有撕裂国家的公民纽带。

paragraphs.en: “Concern with tech monopoly was big in the first Trump administration,” Tim Wu, an antitrust expert and a professor at Columbia Law School who served in the White House under President Biden, told me. “This has been largely forgotten, but the first Trump administration brought the first cases against Facebook, which are under appeal, and against Google, which we won under Biden.” Biden’s Federal Trade Commission and the antitrust division of his Justice Department pushed anti-monopoly policies even harder. The tech giants “wanted to be able to get in and tell us what to do about everything,” Wu said.
paragraphs.zh: 反垄断专家、哥伦比亚法学院教授蒂姆·伍（Tim Wu）告诉我：‘首个特朗普政府时期，科技垄断问题很受关注。’他在拜登总统执政期间曾在白宫服务。‘但这件事后来基本被淡化了。实际上，第一任特朗普政府提起了针对Facebook的首批诉讼（目前仍在上诉中）以及针对谷歌的诉讼，这些在拜登时期我们赢得了胜诉。’拜登的联邦贸易委员会和司法部反垄断司把反垄断政策推进得更猛。伍说，科技巨头‘想要能够进来告诉我们应该如何处理每一件事’。

paragraphs.en: Still, the confrontation between Washington and Silicon Valley under Biden was more rhetorical than substantive. His administration failed to push through any meaningful regulation of the industry, and its legislative achievements in infrastructure, semiconductor manufacturing, and clean energy directly benefited the technology sector. Yet during Biden’s presidency a highly visible element of Silicon Valley turned against the Democrats. It became known as the tech right.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，在拜登执政时期，华盛顿与硅谷之间的对抗更像是口号对抗，而非实质冲突。他的政府未能推动任何有分量的行业监管立法，而且其在基础设施、半导体制造和清洁能源方面的立法成果，直接惠及了科技行业。然而，在拜登总统任内，硅谷出现了一个非常显眼的群体转而反对民主党。他们被称为“科技右翼”。

paragraphs.en: Its most famous figure was Thiel, who had kept a lonely vigil for Trump in Silicon Valley since 2016. But by the early 2020s its most vocal spokesperson was Andreessen. For the tech right, technology is Promethean fire. The founders of the most successful companies in the Valley play a godlike role, for they alone can save America and “Western civilization” from Europe’s hyper-regulated stagnation and from communist and Islamist totalitarianism. Fred Turner, a Stanford professor who studies the culture of technology, told me that deep within Silicon Valley’s libertarianism lies “the idea of a community of saints, of special people, entrepreneurs, philosopher kings.”
paragraphs.zh: 其中最著名的人物是蒂尔，2016年以来他一直在硅谷孤身为特朗普保驾护航。但到2020年代初，它最响亮的代言人是安德里森（Andreessen）。对科技右翼来说，技术就是普罗米修斯之火。谷歌谷中最成功公司的创始人扮演着近乎神祇的角色，因为他们独自能够拯救美国，并将“西方文明”从欧洲过度监管导致的停滞，以及从共产主义和伊斯兰主义极权主义中拯救出来。斯坦福大学研究技术文化的教师弗雷德·特纳（Fred Turner）告诉我，硅谷的自由主义主义深处，潜伏着“圣人共同体”——一群特殊的人、企业家、哲学王的观念。

paragraphs.en: In 2023 Andreessen published a litany of pseudo-Nietzschean credos called “The Techno-Optimist Manifesto.” On AI: “We believe Artificial Intelligence is our alchemy, our Philosopher’s Stone—we are literally making sand think.” The AI revolution is coming, just as electricity did; it will exalt mankind, and any attempt at regulation would be tantamount to mass slaughter: “We believe any deceleration of AI will cost lives. Deaths that were preventable by the AI that was prevented from existing is a form of murder.” Among the “Patron Saints” of this cult of the entrepreneur, Andreessen included John Galt, the hero of every libertarian teen who reads Ayn Rand’s novel Atlas Shrugged, and the 20th-century philosopher James Burnham, best known for predicting that the modern world would be run by an amoral class of “managers,” with the talented few ruling over a mass of semi-slaves. Elsewhere, Andreessen has said that oligarchy is inevitable.
paragraphs.zh: 2023年，安德里森发表了一连串带有“伪尼采”色彩的信条，名为《技术乌托邦宣言》。《关于人工智能》一节写道：“我们相信人工智能是我们的炼金术，是我们的贤者之石——我们正在让沙子字面意义上开始思考。”这场AI革命即将到来，就像电力革命一样；它将提升整个人类，而任何试图加以规制的举动都等同于大规模屠杀：“我们相信，任何放慢AI进程的做法都会让人死亡。那些因本可由AI避免、却因AI被扼杀而造成的死亡，本质上是一种谋杀。”在这场企业家崇拜的“守护圣人”中，安德里森举出了《阿特拉斯耸耸肩》里每个自由主义青少年心中的英雄约翰·高尔特，以及20世纪哲学家詹姆斯·伯纳姆，这位思想家最出名的预言就是现代世界将由一个缺乏道德感的“管理者”阶层主宰，由少数有才华者统治一大群半奴隶。安德里森在其他场合也曾说过，寡头统治是不可避免的。

paragraphs.en: The nearly hysterical voice of “The Techno-Optimist Manifesto” is that of a man who has freed himself from a deeply uncomfortable position. Andreessen was a longtime contributor to Democratic candidates. The political change of Silicon Valley figures like him was less a conversion to Trumpism than a deconversion from liberalism, caused by pressure from below and above. In 2025 Andreessen told The New York Times’ Ross Douthat that the new progressivism of the 2010s had “radicalized” young tech workers, turning them into spiteful and, once COVID hit, indolent rebels who intimidated their white, male, for-profit bosses into bowing to the Great Awokening. Andreessen was willing to pay high taxes and support liberal causes and candidates as long as he was regarded as a hero. But during the past decade, what he called “the Deal”—admiration and a free hand for Silicon Valley in exchange for building great companies, making the world better, and supporting Democrats—was broken, when first young people and then the Biden administration turned against the tech industry.
paragraphs.zh: 《技术乌托邦宣言》中那几乎歇斯底里的语气，其实是一个人从一个极其尴尬的位置里挣脱出的声音。安德里森一直是民主党候选人的长期出资者。像他这样的硅谷人物的政治转向，并非在“特朗普主义”中完成了一次“入教”，而是从自由主义中的“脱离”——既来自下层，也来自上层的压力。2025年，安德里森在接受纽约时报罗斯·道思塔（Ross Douthat）采访时说，2010年代的新自由主义“激进化”了年轻科技从业者，使他们变得怨恨；而一旦新冠疫情来临，又使他们变成了懒散的叛逆者，他们威胁并迫使以白人、男性、营利为导向的老板们向“伟大的觉醒”低头。只要他仍被视为英雄，安德里森愿意支付高税负，也愿意支持自由主义事业和候选人。但在过去十年里，他所谓的“交易”——也就是在硅谷打造伟大公司、让世界变得更好，并支持民主党的前提下，让硅谷享有赞赏和自由度——在年轻人先后以及后来拜登政府转向反科技产业时，已经被打破。

paragraphs.en: According to Andreessen, the administration wanted to kill the entire cryptocurrency sector by keeping the regulatory rules vague while threatening companies with devastating enforcement actions. He also described a meeting that he and his partner were given with senior officials at the Biden White House in May 2024 that, from the point of view of early-stage venture capitalists, was apocalyptic. Regarding AI, Andreessen claimed, the Biden people declared that the whole industry would be limited to a few heavily regulated large companies, with no place for start-ups: Because social media had turned out to be a disaster for democracy, Silicon Valley had to be nationalized or destroyed. Out in the West Wing parking lot, Andreessen and his partner decided to support Trump in that year’s election.
paragraphs.zh: 根据安德烈森的说法，白宫政府想通过把监管规则弄得模糊不清，并威胁企业进行毁灭性执法，以此扼杀整个加密货币行业。他还提到自己与合伙人曾在2024年5月被拜登白宫的高层官员接见，这场会面对早期阶段的风险投资人来说“像末日景象”。谈到人工智能时，安德烈森声称，拜登团队表示整个行业将被限制在少数重监管的大公司手里，初创公司没有立足之地：因为社交媒体已经被证明是对民主的一场灾难，硅谷要么被国有化，要么被摧毁。会后走出西翼停车场时，安德烈森和他的合伙人决定支持特朗普赢得那一年的选举。

paragraphs.en: (I spoke with former Biden officials who disputed what Andreessen claimed he and his partner were told about AI; if anything, the officials said, those present had simply predicted how the capital-intensive technology would play out in the next few years. They pointed to several administration efforts on AI and start-ups that directly contradicted Andreessen’s nightmare account of Biden’s policies. “He needed a conversion story,” one former official told me.)
paragraphs.zh: 我采访过几位前拜登官员，他们对安德烈森所说的“自己和合伙人被告知”的内容表示不同意；那些官员说，至少在场的人讨论的，只是对未来几年高资本密集型技术发展路径的判断而已。他们也指出，政府在人工智能和初创企业方面有若干举措，直接反驳了安德烈森关于拜登政策“噩梦”式说法。一位前官员告诉我：“他需要一个自我叙事上的‘转向’。”

paragraphs.en: In 2020, during the pandemic lockdowns, Sacks and three other venture capitalists started All-In; the weekly podcast would offer market analysis, political argument, and tech-bro banter about poker and cars. It made them famous online, with Sacks (nickname: “The Rainman”) the smartest, most conservative, and least funny of the four. Shortly after January 6, when Facebook and Twitter banned the soon-to-be-former president and other MAGA figures, Sacks stopped talking about Trump as a threat to democracy. Instead, he denounced the “Big Tech oligarchs” who were threatening free speech in “the biggest power grab in history.”
paragraphs.zh: 2020年，在疫情封锁期间，萨克斯与另外三位风险投资人发起了《All-In》节目；这档每周播客会解读市场、展开政治争论，并聊到扑克和汽车等典型“科技男”话题。它让他们在网上声名大噪，而其中萨克斯（绰号“风暴之人”）是四人中最机智、最保守、也最不搞笑的一个。2021年1月6日事件后不久，当Facebook和Twitter封禁了即将失去总统职位的特朗普及其他MAGA人物，萨克斯便不再把特朗普称为民主的威胁。相反，他开始谴责“科技大亨寡头”——他们在他看来正在对“历史上最大规模的权力掠夺”中威胁言论自由。

paragraphs.en: Free speech—at least as it concerned right-wing political figures—was Sacks’s entry point into MAGA, and he never let it go. Anytime one of the “besties” on All-In mentioned January 6, Sacks countered with claims of censorship. His rhetoric became more polemical, a return to his anti-PC youth, but now in the spirit of Trump, not William F. Buckley, as if he was talking himself into a new political identity. At times his enemies were woke oligarchs, at times mid-level technocrats, at times entry-level radicals, but always “elites.” He criticized the elite’s forever wars and trade giveaways to China, and “the collusion between Big Tech and our security state.” He called himself a “populist” and identified with the two-thirds of Americans who are working-class. In 2022, on the Honestly With Bari Weiss podcast, he said, “I think that the next Republican who’s going to be successful has to take a page out of TR’s”—Teddy Roosevelt’s—“playbook here, which is: ‘We do not represent the interests of these oligarchs and these big, powerful companies. We represent the interests of the working man and woman to have the right to free speech, to make a living, to conduct payments. And it should not be up to tech oligarchs to decide who has those rights.’ ”
paragraphs.zh: 言论自由——至少在涉及右翼政治人物时——是萨克斯切入MAGA的入口，而且他从未放弃。每当《All-In》的“闺蜜组合”之一提及1月6日，萨克斯总是用审查制度来反驳。随着他言辞愈发带有论战色彩，这种反击回归了他早年的反“政治正确”姿态，但这次更像是在特朗普的语境中，而不是在威廉·富尔克利式的保守语境里，仿佛他是在对自己“洗牌”成一个新的政治身份。他的对手有时是“觉醒寡头”，有时是“中层技术官僚”，有时是“基层激进分子”，但始终是“精英”。他批评精英们无休止的战争和对华让利的贸易协定，批评“科技大牌与国家安全机器之间的勾结”。他自称是“民粹主义者”，并与美国三分之二的工人阶级认同。2022年，在《Honestly With Bari Weiss》播客中，他说过：“我认为下一个会成功的共和党候选人，必须借鉴TR——西奥多·罗斯福——的打法：‘我们不代表这些寡头和那些庞大强势公司的利益。我们代表的是普通劳动者的利益，让他们有言论自由的权利、谋生的权利、完成交易的权利。到底是不是由科技寡头决定谁拥有这些权利，应该取决于谁？’”

paragraphs.en: From the March 2024 issue: Adrienne LaFrance on the rise of techno-authoritarianism
paragraphs.zh: 题材来自 2024 年 3 月刊：阿德里安·拉弗兰茨（Adrienne LaFrance）关于技术权威主义兴起的报道。

paragraphs.en: If venture-capital populism seems like a stretch, Sacks resolved it this way: End mass immigration of the mentally average, and you’d lay to rest the heartland’s suspicion of Silicon Valley. The solution to inequality is a smaller, less intrusive government, combined with unbridled technological innovation, which would inevitably increase productivity and wages. (Sacks was unaware or unconcerned that decades of unregulated tech and deregulated finance had coincided with growing economic inequality.) “If the Biden administration had only been letting in people with 150 IQs, we wouldn’t have this debate” about immigration, Sacks said on All-In. “If they were just letting in the Elons and the Jensens”—referring to Musk and Jensen Huang, the CEO of the chipmaker Nvidia—“we wouldn’t be having the same conversation today.”
paragraphs.zh: 如果“风险投资民粹主义”听起来像是牵强附会，萨克斯是这么说服他的：停止大量接纳“心理平均”移民，心脏地带才会不再对硅谷心生戒备。解决贫富差距的办法是政府更小、干预更少，再加上不受限制的技术创新，从而必然带来更高生产率和工资。（萨克斯要么没意识到，要么不在乎的是，几十年来放松管制的科技与金融恰恰与经济不平等扩大同步发生。）“如果拜登政府只放进智商 150 的人，我们今天就不会讨论移民问题了，”萨克斯在《All-In》节目中说，“如果他们只放进像埃隆（马斯克）和黄仁勋——我指的是英伟达（Nvidia）首席执行官黄仁勋——这样的人，我们今天就不会有同样的讨论了。”

paragraphs.en: After the Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022, Sacks voiced alarm about the dangers of American involvement in the conflict. Soon he adopted whole hog the “realist” line (which was also the Russian line) that NATO’s eastward expansion had provoked Vladimir Putin into a defensive war. No matter how often Putin claimed Ukraine as a historic part of imperial Russia, how many times he refused to negotiate seriously, how many provinces he annexed, how many Ukrainian civilians the Russian military killed and cities it destroyed, Sacks stuck by his theory. Eventually, it sank him into conspiratorial waters.
paragraphs.zh: 2022 年俄乌战争爆发后，萨克斯开始对美国卷入冲突的风险发出警告。不久后，他便全盘接受了“现实主义”这一论调（也是俄罗斯的说法）：北约向东扩张将普京推向“自卫性”战争。无论普京如何反复声称乌克兰是帝俄历史的一部分，无论他多少次拒绝认真谈判、吞并了多少省份、俄军杀害了多少乌克兰平民以及毁掉了多少城市，萨克斯始终坚持这个理论。最终，这把他带进了阴谋论的深水区。

paragraphs.en: Anne Applebaum: Putin’s newest annexation is dire for Russia too
paragraphs.zh: 安妮·阿普尔鲍姆：普京最新一轮吞并对俄罗斯同样是灾难性打击

paragraphs.en: “This is basically a manufactured conflict that I think really started with Russiagate,” Sacks said in a 2024 speech, “where somehow this fantasy was created that somehow Putin was controlling our elections.” The American left, the “neocons,” and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky managed to fool the U.S. and Europe into risking what Sacks called “Woke War III.” “Somehow, this Russiagate hoax has metastasized into a new cold war with Russia.”
paragraphs.zh: “我认为这基本上是一场被制造出来的冲突，真正是从‘俄罗斯门’开始的，”萨克斯在一次2024年的演讲中说，“因为某种方式下产生了这样的幻觉：普京在控制我们的选举。”美国左翼、“新保守派”以及乌克兰总统弗拉基米尔·泽连斯基设法愚弄了美国和欧洲，让它们冒着萨克斯所称的“觉醒战争第三阶段（Woke War III）”的风险。“不知怎么的，这场俄罗斯门骗局已经发展成了与俄罗斯的新冷战。”

paragraphs.en: It’s worth asking how someone so committed to facts and logic could end up spouting such nonsense. If Sacks made investment decisions on this basis, he would go bankrupt. An obvious explanation is that a successful businessman might not know much about history and politics. But an intellectual deficiency can be compounded by a moral one. It’s striking that the ordeal of a fragile democracy fighting for its life while under assault by an aggressive empire leaves Sacks so cold that he ends up sympathizing with the perpetrator. If you neutralize any sentiment of right and wrong, Ukraine just looks like a risky bet.
paragraphs.zh: 值得追问的是，为什么一个如此看重事实和逻辑的人会说出这样的胡言乱语。如果萨克斯按此逻辑做投资决策，他早就会破产。一个显而易见的解释是，成功的商人未必精通历史和政治。但智识上的缺陷可能被道德上的缺陷进一步放大。令人震惊的是，一个脆弱民主国家在遭受强硬帝国袭击时，正在为生存而战的艰难处境，却让萨克斯如此冷漠，最终竟会对施害者产生同情。如果你抽离善恶判断，乌克兰在他眼里就只是一个风险投资标的。

paragraphs.en: In the 2024 Republican presidential primary, Sacks supported Ron DeSantis—not because Trump had disqualified himself, but because he “just gives his political enemies so much to work with.” A moral objection had become a practical one—so when Trump blew away the Republican field, the final step to complete support was easy. Two weeks after the fundraiser, Trump was invited onto All-In and raved about the splendor of Sacks’s house. Sacks returned the compliment. That July, he delivered a six-and-a-half-minute speech for Trump at the Republican National Convention. By August, he had downgraded January 6 to a long-past event that admittedly “wasn’t great” but had been hyped by Democrats into a “fake coup.”
paragraphs.zh: 在2024年共和党总统初选中，萨克斯支持罗恩·德桑蒂斯，并非因为特朗普“把自己排除在外”，而是因为他认为“特朗普给政治对手留下了太多可供攻击的空间”。一个道德上的反对意见转化成了现实政治上的考量——于是当特朗普清空共和党候选人名单后，公开站到他身后的最后一步便水到渠成。募款活动两周后，特朗普受邀参加“All-In”，在那里大谈萨克斯住宅的宏伟。萨克斯也回以赞赏。那年7月，他在共和党全国大会上为特朗普发表了六分半钟的演讲。到8月，他已将1月6日事件降格为一桩早已过去、尽管“并不理想”却被民主党炒作成“假政变”的事件。

paragraphs.en: Jeff Giesea, a fellow Stanford Review alum and entrepreneur who had been a Trump supporter in 2016 before turning against MAGA, gave me a sympathetic account of the calculus made by Sacks and the tech right. “The story Sacks told himself, I imagine, is that, regardless of Trump’s flaws, the benefits to society from pro-tech policies would be a great improvement over an administration that was mired in safetyism and identity politics,” he said.
paragraphs.zh: 杰夫·吉西娅（Jeff Giesea）是斯坦福评论社的校友，也是企业家。他在2016年是特朗普支持者，后来转而反对MAGA。他给我描述了萨克斯与科技右派背后思路时，语气十分理解。“我想，萨克斯给自己编了这样一个说法：无论特朗普有哪些缺点，支持科技发展的政策给社会带来的好处，也会比一个陷入‘安全主义’和身份政治泥潭的政府要好得多。”

paragraphs.en: Sacks had taken the measure of Trump and found a kindred spirit. After getting to know the ex-president at the fundraiser and on the podcast, he reported his findings: “All of his instincts are Let’s empower the private sector; let’s cut regulations; let’s make taxes reasonable; let’s get the smartest people in the country; let’s have peace deals; let’s have growth. ”
paragraphs.zh: 萨克斯对特朗普做了充分的观察，发现了同道中人。在筹款活动和播客上与这位前总统接触后，他总结道：“他所有的直觉都指向同一个方向：让私营部门掌舵；减少监管；让税负更合理；吸引全国最聪明的人才；达成和平协议；推动经济增长。”

paragraphs.en: Sacks, with J. D. Vance in the foreground, at the Republican National Convention in 2024. A month earlier, Sacks had hosted the fundraiser that helped close the deal on Vance’s selection as Donald Trump’s running mate. (Tom Williams / CQ Roll Call / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 2024年共和党全国大会上，萨克斯与J·D·文斯站在前景中。同样在一个月前，萨克斯主办了那场筹款活动，帮助促成文斯被选为唐纳德·特朗普竞选搭档的最终协议。（Tom Williams / CQ Roll Call / Getty）

paragraphs.en: In December 2024 Sacks was named the White House special adviser for AI and crypto, with a venture capitalist from Andreessen’s firm installed as his deputy. Sacks’s status as a “special government employee” allowed him to stay on as a partner at his company Craft Ventures, while working no more than 130 days over the course of a year at his government job. He also continued as a co-host of his All-In podcast, analyzing technology, influencing market perceptions, making predictions—all while playing a central role in shaping public policy on AI and crypto.
paragraphs.zh: 2024年12月，萨克斯被任命为白宫人工智能与加密货币特别顾问，并由来自安德森公司的一位风险投资家担任副手。作为“特别政府雇员”，萨克斯得以继续留任其公司Craft Ventures合伙人，同时其政府工作在全年内最多仅需出勤130天。他还继续担任“All-In”播客的联合主持人，分析科技、影响市场预期、做出预测——同时又在人工智能与加密货币公共政策的形成中发挥核心作用。

paragraphs.en: Because special government employees are subject to most of the conflict-of-interest rules for regular government employees, the Office of Government Ethics (whose head had been fired at the start of Trump’s second term) required two waivers to allow Sacks to keep a foot in both the public and private sectors. They were written by the White House counsel, David Warrington, a Republican operative who had acted as Trump’s personal lawyer after his first term. A spokesperson for Sacks told The Atlantic, “Mr. Sacks and Craft Ventures had to refrain from investing in companies directly affected by his duties as a government adviser and furthermore had to seek approval from the White House Counsel Office for all potential investments.” In essence, the waivers argued that Sacks’s holdings were so large that keeping dozens of small investments in companies related to crypto and AI would pose no conflict of interest for him, because they made up such a tiny fraction of his overall portfolio. But the waivers give only percentages, and their language is so opaque that it’s impossible to know the actual value of these investments. “They try to finesse the issue by saying, ‘Oh, it’s a relatively small percentage of his portfolio, and he’s so rich, it couldn’t possibly affect him,’ ” Kathleen Clark, an ethics lawyer who teaches at Washington University’s law school, told me, adding that this stance beggars belief.
paragraphs.zh: 由于特别政府雇员同样受到大多数常规政府雇员利益冲突规则的约束，美国政府伦理办公室（该办公室负责人在特朗普第二任期伊始被免职）要求出示两份豁免，允许萨克斯在公共部门和私营部门“各占一脚”任职。两份文件由白宫法律顾问大卫·沃灵顿（David Warrington）起草。沃灵顿是共和党政客，在特朗普第一任期结束后曾担任其私人律师。萨克斯的一位发言人告诉《大西洋月刊》：“萨克斯和Craft Ventures必须避免投资与其政府顾问职责直接相关的公司，并且还必须就所有潜在投资向白宫法律顾问办公室提交审批。” 实质上，这些豁免文件认为萨克斯的资产规模巨大，使他在加密货币和人工智能相关公司中保留数十项小额投资不会构成利益冲突，因为这些投资只占其整体投资组合的极小一部分。然而，豁免文件只写了百分比，而且措辞极其晦涩，实际上无法得知这些投资的真实价值。华盛顿大学法学院的一位伦理律师凯瑟琳·克拉克（Kathleen Clark）告诉我：“他们试图以这种方式回避问题：‘哦，这只是他投资组合中的一个相对小比例，而且他太富有了，不可能对他产生影响。’ 她补充说，这种立场完全难以令人置信。”

paragraphs.en: In November, the Times published a lengthy investigation of Sacks, finding that, despite large divestments, he continued to hold stakes in hundreds of companies that advertised themselves as AI-related, and that key policy decisions benefited both Sacks and his Silicon Valley associates. A chorus of them, including Andreessen, rushed to his defense. Sacks called the Times article a “hoax,” hired a defamation-law firm to write a threatening letter, and argued that he had cost himself and his company a lot of money—$200 million in crypto holdings alone—to work in government voluntarily without pay. Clark waved aside the question of whether there’s personal corruption on Sacks’s part. “I urge you to limit your use of the term conflict of interest,” she told me, “because it doesn’t begin to capture what’s going on.”
paragraphs.zh: 11月，《纽约时报》刊发了一篇对萨克斯的长篇调查报道，结果发现尽管他已经大量套现退场，他仍继续持有数百家自称与人工智能相关的公司股份，而关键的政策决定同时惠及了萨克斯本人及其硅谷同僚。包括安德森在内的一众人士纷纷站出来替他辩护。萨克斯把《时报》文章称为“骗局”，雇了一家名叫诽谤诉讼律所的机构写了封带有威胁意味的信，并辩称自己为了在政府任职做公益、且不领取薪酬，已给自己和公司损失了数额巨大的财富——仅加密货币持仓一项就损失了2亿美元。克拉克对我说，不要去追问萨克斯是否存在个人层面的腐败问题。“我敦促你限制‘利益冲突’一词的使用，因为它根本不足以概括正在发生的事情。”

paragraphs.en: What’s going on is that Sacks joined the most corrupt administration in American history. Throughout his year in the White House, his work on tech policy brushed up against the spectacular grift of his boss at almost every turn. Giesea, the former Stanford Review colleague, who remains an admirer of Sacks, said, “He is an asset to the Trump administration on AI policy. But now he’s trapped in a corrupt clown show.” The pervasive rot makes it almost impossible to distinguish public policy from private venality. The Trump administration’s corruption requires a taxonomy of its own.
paragraphs.zh: 真正发生的，是萨克斯加入了美国历史上最腐败的行政团队。回顾他在白宫的一年任期，在科技政策上的每一步工作几乎都与其上司的离奇敲诈行为擦出火花。前《斯坦福评论》同事、仍然钦佩萨克斯的吉西亚（Giesea）说： “他对特朗普政府的人工智能政策是资产，但现在他却被困在一场腐败的小丑秀里。” 这种弥漫的败坏几乎让公共政策与私人贪腐难以区分。特朗普政府的腐败，已经需要一套属于自己的分类法。

paragraphs.en: George Packer: America’s zombie democracy
paragraphs.zh: 乔治·帕克：美国的僵尸式民主

paragraphs.en: At the most blatant level are the gifts the president accepts from abroad: the $130,000 gold bar and the gold Rolex desk clock from Swiss billionaires, followed by a lowering of U.S. tariffs on Switzerland; the $400 million jet from the Qatari royal family that might cost another half a billion or so to be outfitted as Air Force One, followed by a presidential visit (Trump’s first major foreign trip in his second term) to a country accused of sponsoring terrorism; the Trump-family memecoins sold to wealthy favor seekers. Clark called such brazen bribes “power corruption”: displays intended to show that Trump can get away with anything—“the equivalent of shooting somebody on Fifth Avenue.”
paragraphs.zh: 在最赤裸裸的层面上，就是总统从海外接受的这些“礼物”：来自瑞士亿万富豪的13万美元金币和一只金色劳力士桌面钟，随后是对瑞士降低美国关税；来自卡塔尔王室的4亿美元喷气机，其改装为“空军一号”可能又要再花大约5亿美元，随后是总统对一个被指控为恐怖主义资助者国家的访问（这是特朗普第二任内阁首次重磅出访）；再加上为赢得富裕“关系买手”而出售的特朗普家族纪念币。克拉克把这种公然受贿称为“权力腐败”：一种示范性表演，旨在显示特朗普可为所欲为——“相当于在第五大道开枪打人”。

paragraphs.en: A slightly less glaring kind of corruption abuses government power for private gain: presidential pardons handed out to past and future benefactors; investment deals floated by Trump’s two favorite diplomats, his real-estate buddy Steve Witkoff and his son-in-law Jared Kushner, during the most sensitive peace talks in Russia and the Middle East; major investments in Trump-family crypto and real-estate businesses by foreign governments with extensive U.S. interests; stock trades and prediction bets likely based on insider access to official information, including about war.
paragraphs.zh: 还有一种不那么引人注目的腐败形式，会利用政府权力牟取私利：向过去和未来的受益人发放总统赦免；在俄罗斯和中东最敏感的和平谈判期间，特朗普最宠爱的两位外交官——他的房地产伙伴史蒂夫·维特科夫（Steve Witkoff）和儿媳（女婿）贾里德·库什纳（Jared Kushner）——抛出投资交易；外国政府对与特朗普家族有关的加密货币和房地产企业进行大规模投资，而这些政府又与美国有广泛利益往来；以及可能基于内幕官方信息（包括战争相关信息）进行的股票交易和市场预测投注。

paragraphs.en: Criminal anti-corruption statutes are still on the books. But these embarrassing shows of personal turpitude go uninvestigated and unpunished because the mechanisms for holding public officials accountable have been destroyed. When whistleblowers go unprotected, inspectors general are fired, incompetent loyalists replace nonpartisan civil servants, the Department of Justice is turned into the president’s own law firm and police force, and Congress abandons any oversight function, nothing is left to prevent the rot from spreading into every cell of government. (When Senator Warren wrote to Sacks asking for information on potential conflicts of interest in his role as a special government employee, the answer was silence.) The effect is to demoralize the public, to instill a sense of powerlessness. “We’re living in an era when the corruption is occurring on an unprecedented scale, orders of magnitude larger than anything we’ve seen in the history of this country,” Clark said. “And yet the more important story is what Trump has done to enable that corruption, which is dismantling the rule of law.”
paragraphs.zh: 反腐败刑事法规仍然存在于法典之中。但这些令人尴尬的个人恶行却未被调查、未受处罚，因为监督公职人员的机制已经被破坏。举报人得不到保护，监察长被解雇，非党派文官被不称职的忠诚人士取代，司法部被变成了总统自己的律师事务所和警力，国会也放弃了任何监督职能，那么就没有任何力量能阻止腐败渗透到政府每个细胞。 （当沃伦参议员写信给萨克斯，要求提供他作为“特别政府雇员”可能存在利益冲突的信息时，答复只有沉默。）其效果是使公众士气低落，灌输一种无力感。克拉克说：“我们正生活在一个前所未有规模的腐败时代，这个规模比美国历史上任何时期都大，差距不只是高一个数量级，而是数量级上的差异。” “但更重要的故事是，特朗普所做的事情使这种腐败成为可能——他正在拆解法治。”

paragraphs.en: Finally, there’s what Lawrence Lessig, of Harvard Law School, calls “institutional corruption,” which may be perfectly legal: the warping of public trust toward private ends, the replacement of the country’s priorities with those of a special-interest group. This brings us back to Sacks.
paragraphs.zh: 最后是哈佛法学院的劳伦斯·莱西格（Lawrence Lessig）所称的“制度性腐败”（institutional corruption），它可能完全合法：即将公共信任扭曲到私人目的上去，将国家优先事项替换为某个特殊利益集团的目标。这又把我们带回到萨克斯身上。

paragraphs.en: In his 2025 inaugural address, Trump declared America to be at the start of a “golden age.” His administration put crypto and AI at its center.
paragraphs.zh: 在其2025年就职演说中，特朗普宣称美国正站在“黄金时代”的开端。他的政府把加密货币和人工智能置于核心位置。

paragraphs.en: Cryptocurrency is a long-standing libertarian project—the dream of a privatized financial system. The founders of PayPal originally aspired to create a tool that gave people around the world access to finance, including in poor and corrupt countries without reliable banking institutions. But in practice, crypto’s anonymity and volatility have made it extremely prone to criminal activity and risky speculation. As a candidate in 2024, Trump, a former crypto skeptic and a latecomer to investing in it, won the industry’s lucrative backing on a promise to put the federal government to work on its behalf and turn the U.S. into “the crypto capital of the planet.” Back in office, he pardoned convicted crypto executives, neutered consumer protections, ended investigations by the Securities and Exchange Commission into crypto firms with ties to Trump’s businesses, and disbanded the Justice Department’s crypto-enforcement team. In May 2025, investors paid up to $400 million to buy $TRUMP memecoins in exchange for access to the president at a private crypto gala. Since 2024, Trump’s crypto wealth has grown by at least $7.5 billion.
paragraphs.zh: 加密货币一直是一个长期存在的自由主义理想项目——一种金融体系私有化的梦想。PayPal 的创始人最初的愿景，是打造一种让世界各地的人都能使用金融服务的工具，甚至包括那些缺乏可靠银行体系、贫穷且腐败的国家。但在实践中，加密货币的匿名性和高波动性使其极易成为犯罪活动和高风险投机的温床。2024 年竞选时，特朗普——一位以前怀疑加密货币、且涉足投资偏晚的人——凭借“让联邦政府为其服务、把美国变成“地球上加密货币之都”的承诺，赢得了该行业丰厚的资金支持。上任后，他特赦了因涉案被定罪的加密公司高管，削弱了消费者保护措施，终止了证券交易委员会对与其商业利益相关的加密公司的调查，并解散了司法部的加密执法小组。2025 年 5 月，投资者为购买 $TRUMP 备受追捧的备忘币（memecoin）支付了最高 4 亿美元，以换取在一次私人加密盛会接近总统。自 2024 年以来，特朗普在加密领域的财富至少增长了 75 亿美元。

paragraphs.en: James Surowiecki: Crypto is a victim of its own success
paragraphs.zh: 詹姆斯·苏罗维茨基：加密货币成了自己成功的牺牲品

paragraphs.en: Sacks’s main item of business was to push through Congress a bill that would create a regulatory structure for cryptocurrency—something that the Biden administration hadn’t done, to the frustration of the industry and venture capitalists. The GENIUS Act required issuers of a type of crypto called stablecoin to back their digital currency on a one-to-one basis with assets such as dollars and short-term U.S. Treasury bills. According to Sacks and other supporters, the GENIUS Act would position the dollar as the default currency of the digital economy, while providing guardrails against fraud and other abuses. Critics argued that the guardrails were inadequate, and that crypto issued by private firms with government backing could undermine the entire financial system because of weak regulations and nonexistent enforcement actions. The law also does nothing to prevent government officials from profiting off crypto. When the GENIUS Act passed on a bipartisan vote in July, Silicon Valley and Sacks won the first big return on their investment in Trump.
paragraphs.zh: 萨克斯的主要政务任务，是推动国会通过一项法案，为加密货币建立监管框架。拜登政府未能做到这一点，这使得整个行业和风险投资界都感到沮丧。GENIUS 法案要求发行一种名为稳定币的加密资产的机构，要以一比一方式，以美元或短期美国国债等资产来支持其数字货币。按照萨克斯和其他支持者的说法，GENIUS 法案将使美元在数字经济中成为默认货币，同时设置防止欺诈等滥用行为的安全护栏。批评者则认为，这些护栏远远不够，而且私营企业在政府背书下发行的加密货币可能会因为监管薄弱和执法缺位而削弱整个金融体系。该法案也没有阻止政府官员从加密货币中获利。当该法案在 2025 年 7 月以两党票通过时，硅谷和萨克斯赢得了其在特朗普身上的首笔“投资回报”。

paragraphs.en: If Sacks’s purpose with crypto was to bring it under a federal regulatory regime in order to make the industry more viable to buyers and valuable to investors, his goal with AI was to keep it unregulated, and to align administration policy with the industry’s wishes. His motto became “Let the private sector cook.”
paragraphs.zh: 如果萨克斯在加密领域的目的，是将其纳入联邦监管体系以让行业更适合买家、对投资人更有价值，那么在人工智能问题上，他的目标则是保持其去监管化，并让行政当局的政策与行业诉求保持一致。他的口号变成了“让私营部门自己去做”——“Let the private sector cook.”

paragraphs.en: At the start of his term, Trump revoked a Biden executive order that, among other measures, required AI labs to share the results of safety testing with the government. Though one company found that complying with the order required just one day of work for a single employee per year, Trump pronounced it onerous. Safetyism became a dirty word on the tech right, almost as contemptible as the phrase woke AI—an all-purpose indictment of Biden-era attempts to limit harm from AI to the public, especially children. Yet in the early weeks of the new administration, its policies reflected more continuity than rupture. Not only did Trump keep Biden’s restrictions on licensing the export of advanced AI technology to adversaries such as China; he even strengthened them.
paragraphs.zh: 在任期伊始，特朗普撤销了一项拜登行政令，该令除其他内容外要求人工智能实验室将安全性测试结果与政府共享。尽管有家公司发现，只要为一个员工每年安排一天时间就能完成合规要求，特朗普仍然称其过于繁重。安全主义在科技右翼阵营里成了脏词，几乎和“觉醒AI”一样被视作通用弹药，用来斥责拜登时期试图限制AI对公众（尤其是儿童）造成伤害的举措。然而，在新政府的最初几周，其政策体现的更多是延续而非断裂。特朗普不仅保留了拜登针对向中国等对手出口先进人工智能技术的许可限制；他甚至还进一步收紧了这些限制。

paragraphs.en: Sacks’s influence increased when Elon Musk, his old friend and fellow PayPal mafioso, who was running the Department of Government Efficiency near the czar’s office in the Eisenhower Executive Office Building, walked away from his work of stripping the executive branch. “You see a more conciliatory approach to China emerging only after Musk has his falling-out with the White House,” Oren Cass, the founder of the conservative think tank American Compass, told me. “With Musk out of the picture, I think Sacks certainly became more prominent.” In April 2025, David Feith, a China hawk who was a senior director for technology and national security on the National Security Council, was fired in a larger purge after the right-wing influencer Laura Loomer warned Trump that Feith was disloyal. Soon after, the NSC’s whole technology directorate was eliminated, clearing the way for Sacks to become the loudest voice on tech policy. His goal was to keep AI free of regulation and let the private sector sell the most advanced American technology to the world—even to China.
paragraphs.zh: 当马斯克——这位萨克斯的老友、昔日 PayPal“黑帮成员”——在艾森豪威尔行政办公楼内接近AI“沙皇”办公室的政府效率部里推进削减行政部门权力的工作后，萨克斯的影响力上升了。保守智库 American Compass 的创始人奥伦·卡斯告诉我说：“你会看到对华更为缓和的做法，只有在马斯克与白宫闹掰后才出现。马斯克一旦离场，我认为萨克斯当然变得更为突出。”2025 年 4 月，曾在国家安全委员会负责技术与国家安全的高级主任、偏向强硬对华立场的戴维·费思被解雇，成为更大清洗的一部分；这在很大程度上源于右翼网红劳拉·鲁默向特朗普警告费思不忠。此后不久，国家安全委员会的整个技术司局也被取消，为萨克斯成为科技政策最响亮的声音铺平了道路。他的目标是让AI免于监管，让私营部门向世界出售最先进的美国技术——哪怕是卖给中国。

paragraphs.en: On May 13, Trump scrapped a Biden rule, about to take effect, that would have restricted the global spread of advanced AI technology by dividing countries into three categories of trust, with China fully denied access. (A former White House official called it “the most ‘America First’ rule the Biden administration ever had.”) That same day the president traveled to the Middle East to consummate a deal, which Sacks had helped negotiate, to sell 500,000 AI chips to the United Arab Emirates. This astonishing figure alarmed national-security officials: Some of the chips were likely to end up in China, where strict export controls still applied, and the sale would make it easier for the Emiratis to acquire enough computing power to build their own AI capabilities.
paragraphs.zh: 2025年5月13日，特朗普取消了一项即将生效的拜登规则，该规则本将把各国按三档“信任程度”划分，以限制先进AI技术在全球范围内的扩散，并将中国完全排除在外。（一名前白宫官员称其为“拜登政府迄今最‘美国优先’的规则”。）同一天，特朗普飞往中东，完成了一笔由萨克斯参与谈判的交易：向阿联酋出售50万颗人工智能芯片。这一惊人的数字令国家安全官员感到警惕：部分芯片可能最终流向中国，而中国仍受严格的出口管制；这笔销售也会让阿联酋更容易获得足够的算力，进而建立起自己的AI能力。

paragraphs.en: The smell of corruption hung in the air before Air Force One took off for Abu Dhabi. At the beginning of May, one of Witkoff’s sons had announced that the Emirates’ AI-investment firm would put $2 billion into the crypto exchange Binance, using a stablecoin issued by World Liberty Financial, the crypto company founded by the Trump and Witkoff families. A co-founder of Binance, Changpeng Zhao, was pardoned by Trump after serving four months in a U.S. prison in 2024 for failing to comply with anti-money-laundering measures. In January of this year, The Wall Street Journal reported an even more blatant scandal: A few days before Trump’s inauguration, a powerful Emirati politician known as the “spy sheikh” (almost always photographed wearing sunglasses, even in the Oval Office) had bought a 49 percent share of World Liberty Financial. These deals made the UAE chip sale look like a giant payoff from the administration.
paragraphs.zh: 当空军一号准备起飞前往阿布扎比时，空气中弥漫着腐败的气味。五月初，Witkoff 的一个儿子宣布，阿联酋一家AI投资公司将向加密交易所币安投入20亿美元，资金来源是由特朗普家族和Witkoff家族共同创立的加密公司 World Liberty Financial 所发行的稳定币。币安联合创始人赵长鹏在2024年因未遵守反洗钱措施，被判入狱四个月后获特朗普赦免。今年1月，《华尔街日报》披露了更为赤裸的丑闻：就在特朗普就职前几天，一位被称为“间谍酋长”的强势阿联酋政治人物（据称几乎总是戴着墨镜，即便在椭圆形办公室里也是）购买了 World Liberty Financial 的49%股份。这些交易使得这笔对阿联酋的芯片出售看起来像是对该政府的巨大回报。

paragraphs.en: No one is allowed to be more corrupt than the president, but Sacks may well benefit from Emirati goodwill. The nearly $3 trillion UAE sovereign-wealth fund, of which more than half is controlled by the spy sheikh, offers an immense pot of money for venture capital. Although Sacks had no financial interest in the chip deal that he helped broker, it could put Craft Ventures in a sweet spot for a future round of funding. Is it unfair to point this out? Sacks’s position makes it naive not to. Remaining an investor while serving in an administration rife with graft and shaping policies that could significantly affect present and future deals blurs the line between public and private into indistinction. “It’s hard to disentangle his ideology from his personal interests,” the congressional aide who has followed Sacks closely said. “Maybe they’re one and the same: ‘Let the private sector cook,’ and it just so happens he benefits handsomely from that.” (Sacks’s spokesperson told The Atlantic that future investments “would not be a violation of government-ethics rules. Qualified people would not want to serve in government if it meant permanently giving up their careers.”)
paragraphs.zh: 没有人可以比总统更腐败，但萨克斯或许能从阿联酋的“善意”中获益。这个规模接近3万亿美元的阿联酋主权财富基金——其中超过一半由那位掌管情报网络的酋长控制——为风险投资提供了庞大的资金来源。虽然萨克斯在他帮助撮合的那笔芯片交易中没有任何财务利益，但这可能让 Craft Ventures 在未来一轮融资中处于有利位置。指出这一点是否不公平？萨克斯的处境让人很难不这么说。一个人一边当投资者，一边在充斥利益输送的政府中任职，并参与制定可能深刻影响当前和未来交易的政策，这种做法把公与私的边界几乎抹成了一个样。跟踪萨克斯多年的国会助手说：“很难把他的意识形态和个人利益分开。也许它们是同一个东西：‘让私营部门自行运作’，而他恰好从中赚得盆满钵满。”（萨克斯发言人告诉《大西洋月刊》，未来投资“不会违反政府伦理规定。一个合格的人如果意味着要永久放弃自己的职业生涯，就不会愿意进政府工作。”）

paragraphs.en: On July 23, the White House released its “AI action plan” at an event in Washington co-hosted by the All-In podcast. Trump called out each of Sacks’s “besties” from the show, and they shared the stage with Vice President Vance and other administration leaders. (Susie Wiles, Trump’s chief of staff, had nixed the original idea for All-In to be the sole sponsor, perhaps out of a sense of propriety.) The 28-page plan, “Winning the Race,” called for rapid development of AI technology and construction of data centers so the U.S. can achieve global dominance. It was co-signed by Sacks, but its main author was Dean Ball, a technology researcher who served as a White House adviser for four months last year. Ball pointed out to me that the plan didn’t pose a choice between innovation and safety, nor did it take a position on changes in export controls: “What it does say is we should enforce the chip-export controls that we have more robustly than we currently do.”
paragraphs.zh: 7月23日，白宫在华盛顿与 All-In 播客联合主办的一场活动上发布了其“人工智能行动计划”。特朗普逐一点名了 All-In 节目中的每位“闺蜜式”搭档，并与他站在同一舞台上的是副总统万斯以及其他行政官员。（特朗普首席幕僚长苏西·韦尔斯曾否决过让 All-In 独自担任赞助方的原想法，或许是出于廉洁感。) 这份28页计划《赢得这场竞争》主张加快人工智能技术的发展，并建设数据中心，以便美国赢得全球主导地位。该计划由萨克斯联署，但其主要作者是迪恩·博尔特，他是去年在白宫任顾问仅四个月的技术研究人员。博尔特对我说，这份方案既没有在创新与安全之间制造选择题，也没有对出口管制变化表态：“它要表达的只是我们应该比目前更严格、更有力地执行现有的芯片出口管制。”

paragraphs.en: But Sacks had already undermined this key aspect of the plan. A week before it was released, Jensen Huang, the CEO of Nvidia, the world’s leader in AI-chip production, had announced the resumption of the sale of Nvidia’s H20 chips to China, which the Trump administration had banned in April, before Sacks became the dominant official in tech policy. AI is an industry in which the U.S. has a significant advantage over its main rival. China is able to produce less than 3 percent of U.S. computing power—200,000 chips a year to America’s 12 million or so. Hardly anyone except Sacks was able to explain how the decision to lift the ban on selling chips to China fit with “winning the race” for global dominance, or with an “America First” administration.
paragraphs.zh: 但萨克斯早已破坏了该计划的关键部分。在该计划发布前一周，英伟达（NVIDIA）首席执行官黄仁勋宣布恢复向中国出售英伟达的H20芯片。此前特朗普政府在萨克斯成为科技政策核心人物之前，于4月已禁止该销售。人工智能是美国相较其主要对手具有明显优势的行业。中国每年只能生产不到3%的美国算力，约20万颗芯片，而美国则有大约1200万颗。除萨克斯之外，几乎没人能够解释，放宽对华芯片禁售的决定如何与“赢得竞争”、以及“美国优先”政府的目标相匹配。

paragraphs.en: Read: Trump wasted no time derailing his own AI plan
paragraphs.zh: 延伸阅读：特朗普没有浪费时间就把自己的 AI 计划搞偏了

paragraphs.en: “I would define winning as the whole world consolidates around the American tech stack,” he said on All-In. “If we have 80 to 90 percent market share, that’s winning.” In other words, sell advanced American AI everywhere, including China, to make U.S. technologies and companies dominant. The counterargument, made to me by former Biden-administration officials as well as conservative critics of the Trump-Sacks policy, is that China will never allow itself to become dependent on U.S. technology. Instead, the People’s Republic will do what it’s done in other sectors: steal U.S. technology and innovate its own—the long-term “indigenization” strategy of Xi Jinping, and the reason the regime has prevented Chinese AI companies, which are hungry for American chips, from importing anywhere close to the numbers the Trump administration has made available for sale.
paragraphs.zh: 他在《All-In》节目上说：“我会把赢定义为整个世界都围绕美国的技术栈整合。”他说，“如果我们有80%到90%的市场份额，那就叫赢。”换句话说，就是把先进的美国人工智能技术卖到世界各地，包括中国，让美国技术和美国公司保持主导。对我提出反对意见的人，包括前拜登政府官员以及批评特朗普—萨克斯政策的保守派，都认为中国永远不会让自己对美国技术产生依赖。相反，中华人民共和国会像在其他领域做过的那样：偷取美国技术，并研发自己的技术——这就是习近平长期推行的“技术国产化”战略，也是该政权阻止中国AI公司（这些公司渴求美国芯片）进口接近特朗普政府已向市场放开的数量的原因。

paragraphs.en: “Folks on the pro-export side have a story about how actually selling more of these advanced chips to China will addict them to our technology stack and slow their progress,” Oren Cass said of the Trump-Sacks policy. “I find it a ridiculously inadequate story that never holds up to 10 seconds of scrutiny.” Cass distinguished between an ideological view of U.S.-China competition (“two incompatible systems that can coexist but can’t be integrated in any meaningful way”) and the commercial view that has always been Trump’s, and seems to be Sacks’s. The key figure in moving American tech policy on China to the commercial view was Huang, who was eager to gain greater access to the Chinese market. Sacks now had the clout to accompany the CEO of the world’s richest firm into the Oval Office. “When Jensen comes to town, it elevates Sacks’s stature,” the congressional aide said.
paragraphs.zh: Oren Cass说起特朗普—萨克斯政策时表示：“支持出口一方的人常讲一个故事，认为实际上向中国出售更多这些先进芯片会让他们沉迷于我们的技术栈，从而放缓他们的发展。”他补充道：“我觉得这是个荒谬且不耐推敲的说辞，一经仔细审视，根本站不住脚。”Cass将“中美竞争”的意识形态视角（“两个不可兼容、可以并存却无法在任何有意义层面融合的体系”）与特朗普一贯并且似乎也是萨克斯所坚持的商业视角区分开来。推动美国对华科技政策从意识形态转向商业考量的关键人物是黄某（Huang），他一直想进一步进入中国市场。现在萨克斯有了足够影响力，得以陪同世界最富有企业的首席执行官走进椭圆形办公室。“每当Jensen来华盛顿，萨克斯的身位就会被抬高，”一位国会议员工作人员说。

paragraphs.en: I asked a former White House official with knowledge of the discussions if Sacks had achieved his goal of lifting the ban on selling chips to China simply by sitting down with Huang and a president with a well-known weakness for plutocrats. “Yes. That is exactly what happened,” the former official said. As for Sacks’s motive, “there is not a rational explanation. I think doing favors for Nvidia is the only real explanation, or else he believes Nvidia’s talking points that no one else buys.” (In a letter to The New York Times in November, Sacks’s lawyers wrote that the policies Sacks had advocated for benefited “all American chip companies” and that “Mr. Sacks has independently arrived at his views on chip policy by consulting and reading hundreds of experts in the space.”)
paragraphs.zh: 我向一位熟悉相关讨论的前白宫官员打听，萨克斯是否仅凭和黄以及一位众所周知对富豪极其“亲和”的总统坐下来，就实现了取消对华售芯禁令的目标。那位前官员回答说：“是的。确实就是这样发生的。”至于萨克斯的动机，“我找不到一个理性的解释。我认为替英伟达谋好处，是唯一真正合理的解释；要不就是他相信英伟达的论点，而这些论点是没人买账的。”（11月在《纽约时报》上的一封公开信中，萨克斯的律师写道，萨克斯倡导的这些政策惠及“所有美国芯片公司”，并称“萨克斯先生是通过咨询和阅读该领域数百位专家，独立形成了自己关于芯片政策的观点。”）

paragraphs.en: Even if Sacks is solely motivated by a sincere belief in free-market capitalism, his portfolio companies could now have privileged access to the world’s most coveted computer chips in a market where demand is stronger than supply. “This is why the person who’s regulating AI for the U.S. government shouldn’t also be running a venture-capital firm that has money all throughout the tech industry,” the former White House official said. “Of course he’s picking the winners that in some way benefit him.”
paragraphs.zh: 即便萨克斯完全是出于对自由市场资本主义的真诚信念，他旗下的投资组合公司也可能在一个需求大于供给的市场中，优先获得世界上最抢手的计算芯片。前白宫官员说：“这就是为什么负责为美国政府监管AI的人，不能同时再去经营一家在整个科技产业里到处都投过钱的风投公司。”他说，“当然了，他在挑选那些在某种程度上会让他受益的人和企业。”

paragraphs.en: In December, Huang secured an even more valuable victory when the White House allowed Nvidia to begin selling to China one of its most advanced AI chips, the H200. This was too much for some conservative Republicans on Capitol Hill. Jim Banks, a MAGA-aligned senator from Indiana, had already introduced bipartisan legislation, called GAIN AI, that required Nvidia to put American customers, such as start-up companies and universities, ahead of Chinese companies for its limited supply of AI chips. Sacks, determined to prevent government from limiting tech’s commercial potential, began lobbying hard to keep GAIN AI out of the annual defense-appropriation bill. His efforts to get Republican senators to strip it from their version failed, but when the White House declared its opposition, House Republican leadership killed GAIN AI just before the final vote in December. “What ultimately happened is Jensen talked to the president about this, the dam broke, and Sacks got his way,” the congressional aide told me.
paragraphs.zh: 12月，黄仁勋赢得了更关键的一场胜利，白宫允许英伟达开始向中国出售其最先进的AI芯片之一H200。这个决定令国会山上某些保守派共和党人难以接受。印第安纳州、与MAGA立场接近的参议员吉姆·班克斯此前已提出一项两党法案GAIN AI，要求英伟达在AI芯片有限供应中优先保障美国客户，例如初创公司和大学，而不是中国公司。萨克斯决心阻止政府限制科技的商业潜力，于是开始强力游说，努力把GAIN AI排除出年度国防拨款法案。他试图说服共和党参议员从自己版本的法案中删掉该条款失败，但当白宫公开表示反对后，众议院共和党领导层在12月最终表决前夕放弃了GAIN AI。国会助理告诉我：“最后发生的情况是詹森去找总统谈了这件事，冰山终于崩塌，萨克斯得逞了。”

paragraphs.en: Sacks had less success when the administration tried to get Congress to pass a 10-year moratorium on state AI regulations. The measure lost in the Senate in July, 99–1, but its unpopularity didn’t deter Sacks from trying again. In December, Trump signed an executive order, written by Sacks, that banned states from passing laws to regulate AI. By then, state legislatures had introduced hundreds of bills—chiefly in blue states such as California and New York, but also in Florida, Utah, and Texas—and enacted dozens.
paragraphs.zh: 当行政当局试图推动国会通过一项为期10年的州级AI监管暂停令时，萨克斯就没那么顺利。该提案在7月以99比1在参议院被否决，但并未让萨克斯放弃重试。12月，特朗普签署了一项由萨克斯起草的行政命令，禁止各州制定监管AI的法律。到那时，州议会已提出了数百项法案，主要集中在加利福尼亚和纽约等蓝州，也包括佛罗里达、犹他和德州，并且已有数十项法案正式通过。

paragraphs.en: Sacks’s heavy-handed interventions in Congress on behalf of tech companies did not sit well with some of Trump’s MAGA allies. Stopping the spread of sexual material, protecting children from harmful chatbots, preserving individual privacy, heading off catastrophic threats such as bioterrorism, preventing large-scale unemployment—these things turn out to matter to Americans across the partisan divide. Polls consistently show that a majority fear AI will do more harm than good. Citizens of the world’s AI leader have a more negative view of the technology than those of almost any other country. Appearing on All-In in December, Tucker Carlson gently pointed out to Sacks and his co-hosts that Americans already feel powerless—“and all of a sudden you have a technology that promises to concentrate power still further in the hands of people other than them, and so they’re touchy about it.”
paragraphs.zh: 萨克斯在国会中为科技公司“代言式”出手的强硬干预，让特朗普的一些MAGA盟友并不满意。遏制色情内容扩散、保护儿童免受有害聊天机器人影响、维护个人隐私、提前防范生物恐怖主义等灾难性威胁、避免大规模失业——这些议题事实证明在美国两党之外都很重要。民调一贯显示，大多数人担心AI带来的伤害会多于收益。全球AI领导者所在国家的民众，对这项技术的看法比几乎任何其他国家都更负面。12月在《All-In》节目上，塔克·卡尔森温和地对萨克斯和他的共同主持人说，尽管美国人早已感到缺乏掌控感，“结果突然出现一种技术，看起来又会进一步把权力集中到某些人手里，而不是他们自己手里，于是人们对此非常敏感。”

paragraphs.en: Oren Cass told me, “One of the challenges of the tech right is they are—what’s the opposite of adept?” I offered clumsy. “They are very politically clumsy and don’t have a very good feel for the realities of the American electorate, how politics is conducted, what it takes to be successful.” Steve Bannon, a leader of the populist wing of the MAGA movement, recently told me that Sacks’s efforts on behalf of Silicon Valley are blowing up in his face. “Sacks is the best thing to ever happen to the populist revolt against the oligarchs. His unique blend of arrogance and incompetence has single-handedly delivered humiliating defeat to the AI supremacists.”
paragraphs.zh: 欧伦·卡斯对我说：“科技右翼的一个难题是——它们缺什么，怎么说，是‘不善于政治’的反面吗？”我随口说了个笨拙的词：‘拙于政治’。他补充道：“它们在政治上非常笨拙，对美国选民的现实、政治是如何运作的、要想赢得政治成功需要什么，都没有足够的直觉。”马斯·巴农——MAGA民粹派阵营的一位领袖——最近告诉我，萨克斯替硅谷企业做的那些努力正在反噬他自己。“萨克斯是反对寡头最有力的民粹反抗运动中，迄今为止发生过的最好一件事。”他表示，“他那种独特的傲慢与无能并用的混合，已经单枪匹马让AI至上主义者遭遇了耻辱性的失败。”

paragraphs.en: Sacks and the Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at a private White House dinner for technology and business leaders in September (Brian Snyder / Reuters)
paragraphs.zh: 这是9月白宫一次仅限内阁和科技、商业领袖参加的私人晚宴现场，萨克斯与Meta首席执行官马克·扎克伯格同框。（Brian Snyder / 路透社）

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, AI’s capability is doubling about every four months. It is already changing work and life for millions of people, with the potential to transform fields such as medicine and war. Its inventors spend hundreds of billions of dollars to develop the technology even as they issue dire warnings of its dangers: It might kill us, but we have to make it as powerful as possible as fast as possible. Sacks dismisses or minimizes the potential for harm. In public comments he has claimed that AI isn’t addictive like social media, that productivity gains will more than make up for lost jobs, and that the number of teenage suicides caused by chatbots is small. Because China doesn’t care about things like copyright protection, compensated journalism, and restrictions on export licenses, we can’t afford to either. He accuses skeptics of belonging to the cult of effective altruists—“doomers,” funded by a few anti-AI Big Tech billionaires, who peddle lies to invite global control of the technology for their own financial gain.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，人工智能的能力约每四个月翻一番。它已经在改变数百万人群的工作和生活，并可能改变医学、战争等领域。其发明者们在投入数千亿美元开发技术的同时，也不断发出严峻警告：它可能毁灭我们，但我们必须尽快让它变得尽可能强大。萨克斯则对潜在危害持轻描淡写态度。在公开言论中，他声称人工智能不像社交媒体那样令人上瘾，认为生产力收益足以抵消岗位流失，并且由聊天机器人导致的青少年自杀人数不多。由于中国并不在乎版权保护、付费新闻和出口许可限制这类事情，所以我们也“负担不起”去在意这些。他指责持怀疑态度的人属于“有效利他主义者”教条中的一派——“末日论者”（doomers），是少数反对人工智能的科技巨头亿万富豪在背后资助的，他们传播谎言，借此谋取财务上的私利，并推动对这项技术实施全球控制。

paragraphs.en: One of the doomers, Nate Soares, a co-author of If Anyone Builds It, Everyone Dies, told me: “The lab leaders say this is horribly dangerous, the employees say this is horribly dangerous, the eminent scientists and researchers who developed AI decades ago say this is horribly dangerous. The only people who say ‘Don’t worry’ are the venture capitalists. They’re the ones who stand to profit from it but aren’t close enough to understand it.”
paragraphs.zh: 其中一位“末日论者”奈特·索雷斯（Nate Soares），是《If Anyone Builds It, Everyone Dies》的合著者，对我说：“实验室负责人都说这很危险，员工也说这很危险，几十年前就研制出人工智能的著名科学家和研究人员也说这很危险。唯一说‘别担心’的人，是风投。正是这些人将从中获利，但却离这项技术还不够近，无法真正理解它。”

paragraphs.en: Unlike Andreessen, Sacks doesn’t equate regulating AI with mass murder. But for every concern, he has the same answer: AI is coming, just like the tide. If America doesn’t win the race, China will.
paragraphs.zh: 萨克斯与安德森不同，他并不把监管人工智能等同于“群体谋杀”。但对于每个担忧，他都给出同样的回答：人工智能正在到来，势不可挡，像潮水一样。若美国输掉这场竞争，中国就会赢。

paragraphs.en: Once in government, Sacks learned to adopt his boss’s language and defend the indefensible. He derided “fake news” and called climate change a “hoax,” January 6 prosecutions “lawfare,” the notion of White House corruption “nonsense,” and the killing of two protesters by federal immigration agents in Minneapolis a consequence of “antifa-style operations” intent on thwarting the president’s deportation of “criminal aliens.” He liked Trump’s idea of seizing Greenland and predicted that the war in Iran, which he blamed on “that whole neocon establishment,” would probably be short and decisive because the markets wanted it over and Trump’s political instincts were “impeccable.” But on the threats of censorship, politicized justice, state surveillance, and monopoly power, which had once animated his outrage, and which now came from the Trump administration, he had nothing to say. Sacks had become what he always despised—political.
paragraphs.zh: 一旦进入政府，他就学会了采纳上司的语言，并为无法辩护的事辩护。他贬低“假新闻”，把气候变化叫作“骗局”，把1月6日的起诉称作“法律战”，把白宫腐败的说法说成“胡扯”，并把联邦移民执法人员在明尼阿波利斯打死两名抗议者一事，解释为“反法西斯式行动”——即所谓蓄意阻挠总统驱逐“犯罪移民”的行动的结果。他认同特朗普想要“收复”格陵兰的想法，还预测他认为源于“那套新保守主义核心集团”的伊朗战争很可能很短、很决定性，因为市场想要它，且特朗普的政治直觉“无可挑剔”。但面对审查、政治化司法、国家监控和垄断权力这些此前曾激起他愤怒、而如今却都来自特朗普政府的威胁，他却一句话也说不出来。萨克斯已经变成了他曾经最厌恶的那种人——政治家。

paragraphs.en: From the July 2025 issue: The talented Mr. Vance
paragraphs.zh: 摘自2025年7月刊：《才华横溢的万斯先生》

paragraphs.en: In March, he left his position as AI-and-crypto czar, saying that he had completed his 130 days of service, and returned full-time to Craft Ventures. In December he had moved from San Francisco to Austin, just in time to escape a proposed tax on billionaires that may appear before California voters this November.
paragraphs.zh: 今年3月，他辞去了人工智能与加密货币“总管”一职，表示自己已完成130天的任期，并全职回到Craft Ventures。12月，他从旧金山搬到了奥斯汀，正好躲开了加州可能在今年11月提交给选民表决的亿万富翁财产税提案。

paragraphs.en: Silicon Valley will still have a valuable line to the White House. When Sacks stepped down, he was named co-chair of the President’s Council of Advisors on Science and Technology. Its members include Andreessen, Zuckerberg, Huang, Sergey Brin, Larry Ellison, Michael Dell, a co-founder of a cryptocurrency exchange, the CEO of a semiconductor manufacturer, and a billionaire investor who co-hosts All-In with Sacks. (Among the 15 there is one academic scientist.) This lineup, almost a parody of crony capitalism, signals the final union of America’s interests with those of its wealthiest citizens—tech power fused with state power. The private sector is cooking in Washington.
paragraphs.zh: 硅谷与白宫之间仍将保有一条重要通道。萨克斯卸任后，被任命为总统科技与技术顾问委员会副主席。委员会成员包括安德森、扎克伯格、黄仁勋、谢尔盖·布林、拉里·埃里森、迈克尔·戴尔、一位加密货币交易所联合创始人、一位半导体制造商的首席执行官，以及一位与萨克斯共同主持“All-In”的亿万投资人。（在15名成员中，有一位是学术科学家。）这支阵容几乎成了任人唯亲资本主义的讽刺画面，标志着美国国家利益与最富有公民利益的最终结合——科技权力与国家权力的融合。华盛顿正在烹煮着私营部门的力量。

paragraphs.en: In his year there, Sacks achieved his two central goals: putting the government’s seal of approval on crypto and keeping its hands off artificial intelligence. He was also a founding member of an exclusive MAGA-aligned club in Georgetown, with a fee of $500,000, called the Executive Branch, and he midwifed the creation of an AI-industry lobby, Innovation Council, that plans to spend at least $100 million in support of the Trump administration’s technology policy in this year’s midterm elections.
paragraphs.zh: 在这一年里，萨克斯实现了他两个核心目标：为加密货币赢得了政府的“认可印章”，并且让政府把手从人工智能上放开。他还是位于盖蒂斯堡的一家只对外开放的亲MAGA俱乐部“行政分支（Executive Branch）”的创始成员之一，这个俱乐部的会费高达50万美元；他还协助催生了一个人工智能行业游说团体——创新委员会（Innovation Council），该团体计划在今年的中期选举中，为特朗普政府的科技政策投入至少1亿美元支持。

paragraphs.en: In winning his policy battles, though, Sacks might have lost the war. What Tim Wu calls “the turn away from populism to corruption in tech policy” has alienated important parts of the MAGA coalition from Trump and his rich backers. Steve Bannon says that he and his anti–Big Tech allies are going to make the Innovation Council “the moral equivalent of AIPAC: You take that money and you’re dead.” At some point, an unlikely left-right alliance could unite against the tech oligarchs. “Donald Trump and his administration are using the presidency to make themselves and their billionaire friends richer,” Senator Warren told me, listing Sacks’s policy achievements in crypto and AI. “We are at an inflection point where very powerful AI systems threaten to displace jobs and transform our economy—and we will be living with the consequences for years if Sacks gets his way.”
paragraphs.zh: 尽管萨克斯在政策斗争中占了上风，他可能已经在更大的层面上失利。蒂姆·吴所说的“科技政策从民粹主义转向腐败”，已经让特朗普与其富有支持者的一部分重要MAGA阵营分道扬镳。斯蒂芬·班农表示，他和反“大科技”盟友将把创新委员会做成“反义于AIPAC的道德同位体”：你拿了那笔钱，就等于完了。到头来，一场看似不可能的左右联盟，可能会联合起来反对这些科技寡头。“特朗普和他的政府正在把总统职位当作工具，让自己和亿万富豪朋友更富有，”沃伦参议员对我说，列举了萨克斯在加密货币和人工智能方面的政策成果。“我们正处在一个转折点：极其强大的AI系统正在威胁替代就业并重塑经济——如果萨克斯得逞，我们要为后果承受很多年。”

paragraphs.en: AI could well be the most important issue in the 2028 presidential election. Sacks has moved Trump into the camp of the Silicon Valley saints, selling a world few people actually want to live in, where the state is the handmaiden of industry, wealth accumulates to insider elites tainted by grift, and ordinary people find that they’re losing the last power they have left, over their own minds.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智能很可能成为2028年总统大选中最关键的问题。萨克斯将特朗普带入了“硅谷圣坛”阵营，兜售了一个几乎没人真正想生活在其中的世界：国家成为产业的侍女，财富集中到被剥削与灰色交易玷污的内部精英手中，而普通人发现自己对自身思想和精神最后的控制权也在一点点流失。

paragraphs.en: Every so often, the hosts of All-In remember that staggering quantities of money are pooling upward in America, while discontent roils down below. Suddenly sounding earnest, almost chastened, one of them will call on the group to “fix this inequality gap,” end “ostentatious displays of wealth,” do more in the mode of Carnegie and Rockefeller to benefit the public, maybe even support a wealth tax to stave off the coming class war. But Sacks will have none of it. He alone remains committed to the principle of self-interest. He still believes that capitalism means never having to say you’re sorry.
paragraphs.zh: ‘全部在场’节目主持人不时会想起这样一个场景：美国大量财富正在向上端汇聚，而不满情绪却在下层不断翻涌。突然间，他们会严肃得近乎自责地呼吁大家“弥合这个不平等鸿沟”，结束“炫富”，像卡内基和洛克菲勒那样做更多对公共有益的事，甚至支持一项财富税以防止即将到来的阶级战争。但萨克斯对此拒绝回应。他是唯一坚持“自利”这一原则的人。他依然相信，资本主义的意义就在于你永远不用说对不起。
